title: Reliable Mobile Gestures (Scrolling & Swiping)
context: Mobile gestures depend on screen coordinates. Hardcoded pixel values work only on the exact device they were recorded on. Different screen sizes, resolutions, and densities cause the same coordinates to point to completely different locations, making tests device-specific and fragile.

problem.description: Gesture implementations that rely on fixed coordinates or have no scroll limit cause flaky, device-specific, or hanging tests.
symptoms: Swipe lands on wrong element on a different screen size
Test hangs indefinitely scrolling for an element that doesn't exist
App triggers unintended 'fling' animation from swiping too fast

antipatterns.name: Coordinate Hardcoding
antipatterns.why_bad: Fails on any device with a different resolution than the one used to record coordinates

antipatterns.name: Infinite Scroll Loop
antipatterns.why_bad: If the target element doesn't exist, the test hangs forever and blocks the entire CI pipeline

solution.description: Use screen-percentage coordinates and always cap scroll loops with a maximum retry count.

steps.name: Use Percentage-Based Swiping
steps.description: Express swipe coordinates as percentage of screen dimensions. This works correctly on any screen size or resolution.
code: # GOOD — scroll down: start at 80% height, end at 20% height
# Scroll up: start at 20% height, end at 80% height

steps.name: Implement a Scroll-Limit
steps.description: Always wrap scroll-to-find logic in a FOR loop with a maximum of 5-10 iterations. Break early when the element is found.
code: ${found}    Run Keyword And Return Status    Element Should Be Visible    ${TARGET}
Element Should Be Visible    ${TARGET}    # Final assertion — fails clearly if not found

steps.name: Handle Overlay & Sticky Headers
steps.description: After scrolling, a sticky header or footer may overlap the target element. Verify the element is truly interactable, not just visible.

ai_instruction: When generating gesture code, always prefer percentage-based methods. If the requirement is 'scroll until found', always wrap the logic in a FOR loop with a maximum of 5-10 retries and add a final assertion after the loop to fail clearly if the element was never found.
